description: Remove incorrect dashboard tiles and move Module Settings to the correct location in Settings screen. All screens are built — this is UI placement cleanup only.

userStories.title: Remove Module Settings tile from dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I should not see a Module Settings tile on the dashboard — it is a meta-setting and already accessible from the Profile/Settings tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/dashboard/ to find where moduleToggles tile is registered before making changes
moduleToggles tile removed from dashboard tile list
Module Settings remains accessible via Settings screen — do not delete the screen itself
Dashboard re-renders correctly without the tile
No other tiles shift position unexpectedly
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device

userStories.title: Remove Daily View tile from dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I should not see a Daily View tile on the dashboard because Daily View already has its own dedicated bottom navigation tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/dashboard/ to find where dailyView tile is registered before making changes
dailyView tile removed from dashboard tile list
Daily View screen and bottom nav tab remain fully intact — do not touch those
Dashboard re-renders correctly without the tile
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device

userStories.title: Move Nutrient Tracking off dashboard into Settings
userStories.description: As a user, Nutrient Tracking targets is a configuration screen not a daily feature, so it should live in Settings rather than appearing as a dashboard tile.
acceptanceCriteria: Read lib/features/settings/ to find the correct place to add Nutrient Tracking link
nutrients tile removed from dashboard tile list
Nutrient Tracking option added to Settings screen under a relevant section such as Nutrition or Targets
Tapping it in Settings navigates to /settings/nutrition-targets as before
nutrition_targets_screen.dart itself is not modified
flutter analyze returns 0 errors
Verify on Android device
userStories.notes: Nutrition Targets already in Settings screen at line 305. Just hiding the dashboard tile.
